DESCRIPTION: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
APPROACH: Use a hash map to store elements and their indices. For each element, check if target - element exists in the map.
ADVICE: Watch out for duplicate elements and ensure you return indices, not values.
HINT: Try using a hash map for O(n) lookup time.
FOOTER: Make sure to handle empty inputs.

DESCRIPTION: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
APPROACH: Use three pointers: prev, curr, and next to adjust pointers in a single pass.
ADVICE: Make sure to update the head pointer correctly at the end of execution.
HINT: Can be solved iteratively or recursively.

DESCRIPTION: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
APPROACH: Use a stack. Push opening brackets, and for closing brackets, check if the top of the stack matches.
ADVICE: Make sure the stack is empty at the end.
HINT: Use a hash map to map closing brackets to opening ones.
FOOTER: Stack should be empty at end.

DESCRIPTION: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. Return the maximum profit you can achieve.
APPROACH: Keep track of the minimum price seen so far and the maximum profit obtained by selling at the current price.
ADVICE: Make sure prices array has at least 2 elements.

DESCRIPTION: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists in a one sorted list.
APPROACH: Use a dummy node to simplify list building, then compare heads of both lists iteratively.
ADVICE: Don't forget to append any remaining nodes at the end.
FOOTER: Merged list should have all nodes.
